text: Rare earth elements and their application to high performance permanent magnets This paper briefly discusses the role of the rare earth elements (REEs) in today’s global economy, with focus given to their application to high performance permanent magnets that contain neodymium (Nd), praseodymium (Pr), dysprosium (Dy) and cerium (Ce) with small amounts of gadolinium (Gd). These permanent magnets are primarily used within the motor and subsequently the magnet industry is the largest of the rare earth applications by volume. This paper uses data from industry analyst Roskill and Wood Mackenzie, and uses market analysis to calculate that applications of permanent magnets are forecast to make up some 40% of rare earth element demand in 2030. This article explores the supply chain in the rare earth elements sector for permanent magnets for the following countries: United States of America, Europe, Japan and China.
explanation: The passage has a normal scholarly tone with plausible, industry-specific details (REEs, Nd/Pr/Dy/Ce; Roskill/Wood Mackenzie; 40% of demand by 2030), which is consistent with human writing; no strong AI-detection artifacts are evident.
